THIS TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT THE WEST EDGE OF TOWN AND TRACKED INTERMITTENTLY TO THE NORTHEAST FOR ROUGHLY 4 MILES TO AROUND 3 SW OF DAZEY BY 531 PM CDT. THE TORNADO TORE SECTIONS OF ROOFING OFF OF A HOUSE AND STEEL ROOFING OFF OF A GRAIN STORAGE BUILDING. IT DESTROYED ONE SMALL SHED AND IMPALED WOOD PANELS INTO THE SIDE OF A HOME. IT LATER SNAPPED TREES AND LARGE BRANCHES IN SHELTER BELTS ALONG IT PATH. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 108 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 150 YARDS.
TORNADO REPORTED. BUILDING DAMAGE OCCURRING.
SECTIONS OF METAL CATTLE FENCING WERE BLOWN DOWN AT THE END OF A STOCK PEN. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

A VERY THIN AND ROPEY FUNNEL WAS VIEWED FOR SEVERAL MINUTES FROM MULTIPLE LOCATIONS AROUND THE SOUTH END OF LAKE ASTABULA RESERVOIR. AN APPARENT BRIEF TOUCHDOWN WAS NOTED AS LARGE BRANCHES WERE FLUNG EASTWARD SOME 100 YARDS FROM A SMALL STAND OF TREES. NO OTHER DAMAGE WAS NOTED IN THE AREA TO THE WEST OF THE LAKE ALONG 114TH AVE SE. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 75 MPH. MAXIMIMUM WIDTH TO 75 YARDS. TRACK LENGTH LESS THAN ONE QUARTER MILE EASTWARD AND ENDING BY 612 PM CDT.

THIS TORNADO WAS LIKELY WRAPPED IN DOWNBURST WINDS, HEAVY RAIN, AND HAIL AS IT TRACKED TO THE NORTHEAST. IT APPEARS TO HAVE TRACKED FOR UP TO 5 MILES BEFORE IT LIKELY DISSIPATED ABOUT 2.5 MILES NORTH OF HOPE BY AROUND 228 PM CDT. THIS TORNADO SNAPPED POPLAR TREES IN A SHELTER BELT AND SCATTERED LARGE BRANCHES ACROSS CR5 AND INTO AN ADJACENT FIELD FOR UP TO 300 YARDS. MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 150 YARDS. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 85 MPH.

IT APPEARS TO HAVE TRACKED FOR UP TO 5 MILES BEFORE IT LIKELY DISSIPATED ABOUT 2.5 MILES NORTH OF HOPE BY AROUND 628 PM CDT. THIS TORNADO SNAPPED POPLAR TREES IN A SHELTER BELT AND SCATTERED LARGE BRANCHES ACROSS CR5 AND INTO AN ADJACENT FIELD FOR UP TO 300 YARDS. MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 150 YARDS. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 85 MPH.

A FEW LARGE TREE BRANCHES ANDLIMBS BROKEN DOWN ON SHELTER BELTS IN NORTHEAST BUXTON TOWNSHIP. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

THE FIRST OF TWIN TORNADOES VIEWED AND PHOTOGRAPHED FROM RESIDENTS NEAR FLOM AND WAUBUN. THIS TORNADO TRACKED INTERMITTENTLY TO THE NORTHEAST FOR OVER 7 MILES TO AROUND 4 SSE ON MAHNOMEN BY 736 PM CDT. IT BROKE LARGE TREE BRANCHES AND LIMBS IN SEVERAL SHELTER BELT LOCATIONS ALONG ITS PATH AND APPEARS TO HAVE CAUSED A PEAK WIND OF 73 MPH RECORDED AT BOTH THE MAHNOMEN AIPORT AND A NEARBY MNDOT RWIS SENSOR AS IT DISSIPATED. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 80 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 200 YARDS.

THE FIRST OF TWIN TORNADOES VIEWED AND PHOTOGRAPHED FROM RESIDENTS NEAR FLOM AND WAUBUN. THIS TORNADO TRACKED INTERMITTENTLY TO THE NORTHEAST TO AROUND 4 SSE ON MAHNOMEN BY 736 PM CDT. IT BROKE LARGE TREE BRANCHES AND LIMBS IN SEVERAL SHELTER BELT LOCATIONS ALONG ITS PATH AND APPEARS TO HAVE CAUSED A PEAK WIND OF 73 MPH RECORDED AT BOTH THE MAHNOMEN AIPORT AND A NEARBY MNDOT RWIS SENSOR AS IT DISSIPATED. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 80 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 200 YARDS.
SMALL GARDEN SHED BLOWN DOWN. SEVERAL LARGE TREE BRANCHES BROKEN DOWN AROUND A HOMESTEAD SITE.

THIS SECOND OF TWIN TORNADOES IN SOUTHWEST MAHNOMEN COUNTY APPEARS TO TOUCHED DOWN AFTER THE FIRST AND TO HAVE TRACKED NEARLY DUE EAST FOR ROUGHLY 3.5 MILES TO AROUND 3NNE OF WAUBUN BY 735 PM CDT. THIS TORNADO SNAPPED A LARGE SPRUCE TREE AND SEVERAL POPLAR TREES IN ONE FARMSTEAD. THE MAHNOMEN SHERIFFS OFFICE RECEIVED MULTIPLE REPORTS OF TOUCHDOWNS NEAR WAUBUN. TIME BASED ON REPORTS, VIDEOS, AND RADAR. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 95 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 200 YARDS.
MAHNOMEN SHERIFFS OFFICE RECEIEVED MULTIPLE REPORTS OF A TOUCHDOWN NEAR WAUBUN TIME BASED ON PHONE CALL TO THE SHERIFFS OFFICE WITH LOCATION ESTIMATED VIA RADAR

FIRST REPORTED AS A FUNNEL BY SPOTTERS NEAR THE SAND HILLS GOLF COURSE, THIS TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST NORTH OF THE NORMAN COUNTY LINE AND TRACKED TO THE ENE FOR AROUND 3.6 MILES, ENDING ABOUT 6 E OF FERTILE BY 745 PM CDT. NUMEROUS LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED ALONG THE PATH WITH MACHINERY TOSSED, A ROOF TORN OFF A POLE SHED, SEVERAL STEEL GRAIN BINS TOSSED AND CRUMPLED, AND A BARN COMPLETELY COLLAPSED AT ONE FARMSTEAD. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 115 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 400 YARDS.

FARGO DISPATCH REPORTS WATER LEVELS ARE RECEDING. WITHIN THE PAST FEW HOURS, SEWERS BACKED UP IN DILWORTH AND AROUND A FEW DOZEN CARS WERE STALLED OR STRANDED ON ROADS DUE TO HIGH FLOOD WATERS COVERING STREETS IN PARTS OF THE FARGO MOORHEAD METRO. PORTIONS OF ABOUT 6 OR 7 ROADS WERE IMPASSABLE.

THIS TORNADO IS THE FIRST WHICH FORMED IN A CYCLIC SERIES. IT TRACKED FOR ABOUT 1.5 MILES TO AROUND 4NNW WALCOTT BY 524 PM CDT. THE TORNADO BROKE DOWN NUMEROUS TREE BRANCHES IN TREED AREAS ALONG ITS PATH, AND SNAPPED ASH AND COTTONWOOD TREES AS IS CROSSED 57TH STREET SE, ROUGHLY 4 MILES SOUTH OF KINDRED ND. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 95 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 100 YARDS.
A BRIEF TOUCHDOWN WAS NOTED.

A BRIEF TOUCHDOWN WAS OBSERVED IN AN OPENED FIELD, FROM A LOCATION JUST EAST OF THE GREAT BEND EXIT OFF I-29. THIS TORNADO WAS THE FIRST THAT FORMED IN A SECOND OF TWO CYCLIC SERIES, BEGINNING IN RICHLAND COUNTY. THE TORNADO LIKELY TRACKED TO THE NORTHEAST FOR ONE QUARTER MILE OR LESS AND HAD LIFTED BY 531 PM CDT. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 70 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 50 YARDS.
BRIEF TOUCHDOWN OBSERVED FROM INTERSTATE 29.

TWIN FUNNELS MOVED ACROSS THE RED RIVER NORTH OF BRECKENRIDGE AND PRODUCED AT LEAST ONE DAMAGING TOUCHDOWN PATH. THIS TORNADO WAS SECOND WHICH FORMED IN THIS CYCLIC SERIES. IT TOUCHED DOWN NEAR HIGHWAY 75 AND TRACKED NORTHEASTWARD FOR NEARLY 2 MILES TO ABOUT 4 NNE OF BRECKENRIDGE BY 603 PM CDT. THE TORNADO BROKE NUMEROUS LARGE TREE BRANCHES AND LIMBS IN SHELTER BELTS ALONG ITS PATH. ONE FARMSTEAD HAD A FEW ASH AND COTTONWOOD TREES SNAPPED. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 95 MPH. MAX WIDTH TO 100 YARDS.

REPORT AND VIDEO SHOW INTERMITTENT GROUND CONTACTS AS THIS TORNADO THE SECOND TORNADO IN THIS CYCLIC SERIES. IT TRACKED TO THE NORTHEAST FOR ABOUT A HALF MILE OR SO, OVER MAINLY OPEN FIELDS. THE TORNADO LIKELY ENDED BY 6PM CDT. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 75 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 75 YARDS.
REPORT AND VIDEO SHOW INTERMITTENT GROUND CONTACTS FOR ABOUT A HALF MILE OR SO OVER MAINLY OPEN FIELDS. THE TORNADO LIKELY ENDED BY 6PM CDT.
TORNADO ROPING OUT NNE OF WAHPETON OR NORTH OF BRECKENRIDGE.

THE TORNADO IS THE THIRD TORNADO FORMED IN A CYCLIC SERIES. IT TRACKED FOR ABOUT A MILE TO ABOUT 8SW ROTHSAY BY 623 PM CDT. AT ONE POINT IT SNAPPED SEVERAL ASH TREES IN A FIELD SHELTERBELT. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 105 MPH WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 150 YARDS.

THIS TORNADO WAS THE THIRD TORNADO IN A CYCLIC SERIES. IT WAS LIKELY A PARTIALLY RAIN WRAPPED TORNADO AS IT TRACKED INTERMITTENTLY TO THE NORTHEAST FOR NEARLY 3 MILES, TO ABOUT 4 MILES WSW OF HAWLEY BY 638 PM CDT. THE TORNADO BROKE DOWN NUMEROUS POPLAR AND ASH TREE BRANCHES ALONG ITS PATH, AND CRACKED AT LEAST ONE POWER POLE. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 85 MPH AND MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 150 YARDS.

THIS TORNADO TRACKED INTERMITTENTLY FOR ROUGHLY 2 MILES, ENDING IN CENTRAL ERHARD, BY ABOUT 651 PM CDT. THE TORNADO BROKE DOWN NUMEROUS LARGE TREE BRANCHES AND LIMBS IN SHELTER BELTS AND TREE CLAIMS ALONG ITS PATH. AN ASSORTMENT OF ASH AND ELM TREES WERE SNAPPED IN ERHARD. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 100 MPH. MAXIMUM WITH TO 150 YARDS.

A PERSISTENT FUNNEL CLOUD WAS VIEWED FOR A FEW MINUTES BY AN OBSERVER LOCATED ABOUT 6 WSW OF NORRIS CAMP. THIS TORNADO SNAPPED SEVERAL POPLAR, SPRUCE, AND TAMARAC TREES LEAVING A 50 YARD WIDE GOUGE IN THE FOREST AND ACROSS FAUNCE ROAD SOUTH. IT TRACKED INTERMITTENTLY FOR ROUGHLY 15 MILES TO THE EAST NORTHEAST, TO ABOUT 9S OF BAUDETTE BY 510 PM CDT. THIS AREA IS DEEP IN THE BELTRAMI ISLAND STATE FOREST AND IS LARGELY INACCESSIBLE. HOWEVER SIMILAR TREE SNAP AREAS WERE NOTED ACROSS PILOT GLADE ROAD, ABOUT 10S OF PITT,AND ACROSS CR1 ABOUT 5N OF CARP. PEAK WIND TO 105 MPH. MAX WIDTH TO 100 YARDS.

A TORNADO TRACKED INTERMITTENTLY FOR AROUND 4 MILES TO THE EAST NORTHEAST TO ABOUT 2.5 WNW MICHIGAN BY 232 PM CDT. IT WAS VIEWED AND REPORTED AS A FUNNEL CLOUD BY SPOTTERS LOCATED AT VARIOUS SPOTS ALONG OR NEAR U.S. HIGHWAY 2. THE TORNADO SNAPPED A COUPLE OF MATURE ASH TREES AND TORE OFF SHINGLES AT ONE FARMSTEAD. IT LATER DESTROYED A SMALL POLESHED AT A SECOND FARMSTEAD. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 95 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 100 YARDS.
